item.title: Bank of France to Raise 2026 Inflation Forecast, Moulin Says
item.description: France’s central bank will increase its projection for consumer-price gains this year due to the Iran war, central bank Governor Emmanuel Moulin said.

item.title: JPMorgan Sees Stocks Powering Through Any Short, Sharp Pullbacks
item.description: US stocks have further to run as corporate earnings growth underpins sentiment despite some signals suggesting equities may have risen too far, JPMorgan Asset Management’s Jack Caffrey said.

item.title: Europe’s Space Race Gains Pace With Iceye’s Jump to €10 Billion Valuation
item.description: Finnish satellite-intelligence company is one in an ecosystem of space-based industries that’s emerging in Europe

item.title: US Trade Gap Narrows as Oil Exports Offset AI-Driven Imports
item.description: The US trade deficit narrowed in April as a surge in oil exports helped offset ongoing increases in imports of equipment powering the data center buildout.

item.title: Canada Trade Surplus Widens to Largest Since Before Tariffs
item.description: Canada’s trade surplus widened in April as crude oil drove exports to a record high, while gold shipments declined sharply.

item.title: Mexico Inflation Slowed Much More Than Expected in May
item.description: Mexican inflation slowed much more than expected in May and back into the central bank’s target range, substantiating policymakers’ decision to again cut interest rates at their meeting last month.

item.title: Monroe’s Koenig Warns 401(k) Cash Risks More Private Market Pain
item.description: The potential flow of 401(k) retirement money into private markets risks putting managers under pressure to invest quickly and may end up exacerbating demand for redemptions, said Monroe Capital’s chief Ted Koenig.

item.title: BP CEO Shakes Up Structure in Push Back to Oil and Gas
item.description: New BP Plc Chief Executive Officer Meg O’Neill reorganized the oil giant’s leadership and reporting structures in an overhaul that cements the company’s focus on oil and gas as it tries to move beyond recent boardroom drama.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Says EU Bank M&A Rationale Is Rising With Profits
item.description: The rationale for mergers and acquisitions among European banks is rising even in the face of hurdles to cross-border tie ups, according to one of the region’s top financials deal-makers.

item.title: World's Hottest Space Stock Posts Rally That Dwarfs All Rivals
item.description: Shares of Satellogic, which builds satellites in Uruguay, are up more than 250% this year.

item.title: Clip Partners With Ant on Consumer Payments Wallet in Mexico
item.description: Mexican payments company Clip is launching a digital wallet as it pushes to bring millions of consumers and small businesses into the country’s formal financial system.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 9, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.4% at 7:51 a.m. in New York as a rebound in AI-linked names continues.

item.title: Big Oil Balks at Trump’s Invitation to Drill in Alaska’s Arctic Wildlife Refuge
item.description: The specter of shifting politics deters drillers from taking up new licenses.

item.title: Goldman, Barclays Traders Warn of Market Risks After Friday Rout
item.description: A selloff on Friday whipsawed investors used to seeing momentum stocks go nowhere but up. They shouldn’t be quick to disregard it as a one-off.

item.title: Pakistan’s Budget Faces Further Delay, Prompting Speculation
item.description: Pakistan has again pushed back the presentation of its annual budget, further extending an unusual delay that has prompted speculation from analysts and investors about its reasons.

item.title: Klarna Launches US Savings Accounts to Attract Everyday Users
item.description: Klarna Group Plc is launching savings accounts in the US as the digital lender looks to deepen its core banking business and attract more everyday users.

item.title: Smucker Profit Tops Expectations, Buoyed by Coffee Prices
item.description: Jelly and coffee maker JM Smucker Co. posted fourth-quarter profits that beat Wall Street expectations as higher prices helped boost the packaged food company.

item.title: Ad Agencies Are a Buying Opportunity in AI Era, Berenberg Says
item.description: Investor jitters about the potential disruption caused by artificial-intelligence adoption has created a broad-based buying opportunity for the shares of European advertising agencies, according to Berenberg analysts.

item.title: South Africa Targets Key Mining Registry by End of March
item.description: South Africa expects to fully launch a long-awaited database of mineral rights within 10 months, according to a senior official.

item.title: Japan Inc.’s Shake-Up Sparks Flashbacks to Dot.com Boom and Bust
item.description: The rise to the top by AI-related companies has parallels to an earlier bubble

item.title: CoreWeave Plans European Investor Calls For Potential Debt Sale
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. is to hold calls with European high-yield investors on Tuesday as the AI infrastructure provider considers financing transactions that may include dollar and euro bonds.

item.title: Ten Years On, the UK Is Counting the Economic Cost
item.description: A decade after the Brexit vote, the UK is assessing the damage and debating how to unwind it.

item.title: Booming AI Demand Fuels Jump in China’s Exports
item.description: Bloomberg Economics cites link between global tech supercycle and trade flows

item.title: Standard Bots Raises $200 Million to Manufacture Robots in US
item.description: Standard Bots has raised $200 million in a new round of funding to ramp up manufacturing of robotic arms in the US as the country vies to keep pace with China in building more advanced robots.

item.title: Crop and Fertilizer Markets Wipe Out Iran War Risk Premium
item.description: The Iran war risk premium that swept through crop and fertilizer markets is rapidly evaporating as fears of prolonged supply disruptions fade, easing one of the biggest threats to food inflation.

item.title: Robots May Help Defuse US-China Tensions
item.description: The two countries have vital tech for the coming wave of robots

item.title: Sugar Swings as Traders Weigh Brazil Output Against Deficit Risk
item.description: Sugar futures fluctuated in New York as traders weighed ample Brazilian supplies against mounting El Niño risks that could tighten global supplies later this year.

item.title: Blackstone Is Buying Up SRTs as Banks Rush to Hedge Loan Risks
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is pushing forward in the fast-growing market for significant risk transfers as banks hedge possible losses in their swelling loan books.

item.title: Philippines’ PLDT Seeks Up to $400 Million From REIT Listing
item.description: Top Philippine telecoms firm PLDT Inc. plans to raise between $300 million and $400 million from the listing of a real estate investment trust for its data center business amid a global wave of investor interest in technology and artificial intelligence assets.

item.title: Taiwan Mulls Curbs on AI Chip Exports to China to Align With US
item.description: Taiwan authorities are considering much stricter export controls on AI chip sales to China to further align with US measures, according to people familiar with the matter, an effort to address semiconductor smuggling that risks drawing a rebuke from Beijing.
